comments.text: Since the 25-pound problems are listed at 44 dollars, this 100-pound problem should probably be worth about 176 dollars. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

I think I'll keep this as \$100, which is the prize offered for establishing $o(n^2)$, which is the most common way this problem was posed. The (greater) prize of £100 is already listed for the (harder) problem of $O(n^{3/2})$ in the remarks.

Then maybe this page should explicitly mention that there is also a $100 prize that is distinct from the £100 prize so that people aren't fooled into thinking that you just made an error with the currency. I'm not the only one who did so; Tao's list has it wrong as well.
Erdős also asked this question here on page 18. (This source is [Er83b].) (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
